title: Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles
summary: A rapid shelter sequence that moves people and animals away from glass, stops outdoor travel, and reduces avoidable damage without sending anyone into the storm.
content:
A rapid shelter sequence that moves people and animals away from glass, stops outdoor travel, and reduces avoidable damage without sending anyone into the storm.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Bring people and animals indoors before hail begins and close shutters or curtains without standing directly beside windows.
Move to an interior area and protect head and eyes if glass breaks; keep pets restrained and livestock in their safest existing shelter.
If driving, slow gradually, increase distance, switch on lights, and stop only at a safe off-road location or sturdy cover that is not flood-prone.
After the storm, avoid broken glass, damaged roofs, fallen cables, and stressed animals until hazards are checked.
warnings: Do not run outside to cover a vehicle once hail has started.
Do not shelter under isolated trees or weak awnings.
common_mistakes: Starting Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Hailstorm shelter for people, animals, windows, and vehicles.']
